note: Third retro today (sequence 3). No new c:\dev commits since sequence 2 (still 142/window, contributor count drifted +1 on a window-boundary commit, not new work) -- this entry's session_focus covers a separate repo (charlie-deep-research) not reflected in c:\dev's own commit count.

session_focus.summary: CIC documentary archival verification: resumed a locked, re-verified provenance sweep (39 units, 37 NEEDS_REVISION), built a remediation plan + two keystone sourcing packets (V-5.3 hotel-room sketch, V-6.5 1944 exit mechanics), then did live verification of the user's Benson Ford Research Center box list against the real online finding aids -- fetched and text-extracted 9 separate PDFs via WebSearch/WebFetch/pdftotext, catching 3 mislabeled boxes and 1 wrongly-dropped accession, surfacing 4 new independent-witness leads. Closed by correcting a fabricated Jan-March 1944 chronology in a pasted research checklist and rebuilding it around 4 real dated anchors, shipped as both a repo doc and a published web artifact for on-site reference.

session_focus.verification_result: 9 of 15 originally-listed archive boxes confirmed exact against live finding-aid PDFs; 3 boxes (Acc.38 #51/54/56) found mislabeled -- claimed 'Alphabetical Files'/'Personal Records' content, actually an unrelated Subject Files subsubseries; Acc.823 (P.E. Martin papers) was wrongly marked unlocatable on first pass, corrected via a broader keyword search rather than filename-guessing. Final list: 15 confirmed boxes, both keystones covered 3-4 candidates deep.
process_learnings: PDF text extraction: WebFetch cannot parse binary PDF streams -- fetch saves the binary locally, then `pdftotext file.pdf -` via Bash reliably extracts it. This unlocked verifying 9 separate archive finding-aid PDFs in one session.

Archive site search caveat: findingaids.thehenryford.org's `?keyword=` search is not accession-number-aware -- searching 'Acc.833' returned an unrelated record. Broader term searches (e.g. 'sorensen') surfaced accessions that targeted filename-guessing and PDF-name searches missed entirely (Acc.823, Acc.13) -- broaden the search before concluding something doesn't exist.
Two-column archival PDFs can misorder box-header vs. content in text extraction -- box attribution near a page break carries +/-1 box uncertainty; flag rather than assert exact.
drift_caught: User-pasted 'verification results' for the 37 NEEDS_REVISION items were fabricated placeholder content (generic IDs like V-9.1a, an invented 4th defect category) that didn't match the real, already-verified consolidated file -- caught by comparing against the actual file content read earlier in-session rather than trusting the paste.

A separately pasted 'arrival-day checklist' invented a Jan-March 1944 confrontation chronology with no source, misattributed the Treatment's cited p.912 passage to that invented window, and proposed a non-existent 'read stress from pencil pressure' forensic technique -- caught before building the artifact from it, rebuilt around the 4 dates that actually have documentary support.
